420			_ = in.Store.ParkToProposed(in.Bead.ID, bead.ParkReviewRequestClarification, nil)
421			report.Status = ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestClarification
422			report.Detail = "pre-close review: REQUEST_CLARIFICATION"
423			out.Approved = false
424		}
425		if reviewRes.Verdict == VerdictBlock || reviewRes.Verdict == VerdictRequestChanges {
426			_ = applyReviewTriageDecision(in.Store, in.Bead.ID, in.Assignee, now().UTC(), report.PowerClass)
427		}
428	
429		out.Report = report
430		return out
431	}
432	
433	func runPreCloseReviewGroup(ctx context.Context, reviewer BeadReviewer, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewGroupResult, error) {
434		if reviewer == nil {
435			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: reviewer required")
436		}
437		if groupReviewer, ok := reviewer.(reviewGroupReviewer); ok {
438			return groupReviewer.ReviewGroup(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl)
439		}
440		result, err := reviewer.ReviewBead(ctx, beadID, resultRev, impl)
441		group := &ReviewGroupResult{
442			BeadID:    beadID,
443			ResultRev: resultRev,
444			Slots: []ReviewGroupSlotResult{
445				{
446					ReviewerIndex: 0,
447					Result:        result,
448				},
449			},
450		}
451		if err != nil {
452			group.Slots[0].Error = err.Error()
453		}
454		return group, err
455	}
456	
457	func reviewErrorReviewerIndex(group *ReviewGroupResult, res *ReviewResult) (int, bool) {
458		if group == nil || len(group.Slots) < 2 {
459			return 0, false
460		}
461		if res != nil {
462			for _, slot := range group.Slots {
463				if slot.Result == res {
464					return slot.ReviewerIndex, true
465				}
466			}
467			return res.ReviewerIndex, true
468		}
469		for _, slot := range group.Slots {
470			if slot.Error != "" {
471				return slot.ReviewerIndex, true
472			}
473		}
474		return 0, true
475	}
476	
477	func reducePreCloseReviewGroup(group *ReviewGroupResult) (*ReviewResult, error) {
478		if group == nil || len(group.Slots) == 0 {
479			return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group: no review slots")
480		}
481		var firstApprove *ReviewResult
482		var firstNonApprove *ReviewResult
483		var firstResult *ReviewResult
484		for _, slot := range group.Slots {
485			if slot.Result != nil && firstResult == nil {
486				firstResult = slot.Result
487			}
488			if slot.Error != "" && slot.Result == nil {
489				if firstResult != nil {
490					return firstResult, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: %s", slot.ReviewerIndex, slot.Error)
491				}
492				return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: %s", slot.ReviewerIndex, slot.Error)
493			}
494			if slot.Result == nil {
495				if firstResult != nil {
496					return firstResult, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: missing review result", slot.ReviewerIndex)
497				}
498				return nil, fmt.Errorf("review-group slot %d: missing review result", slot.ReviewerIndex)
499			}
500			res := slot.Result
501			switch res.Verdict {
502			case VerdictApprove:
503				if !reviewResultHasACEvidence(res) {
504					return res, fmt.Errorf("%w: approve verdict without per-AC evidence", ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable)
505				}
506				if firstApprove == nil {
507					firstApprove = res
508				}
509			case VerdictRequestChanges, VerdictBlock:
510				if firstNonApprove == nil {
511					firstNonApprove = res
512				}
513			default:
514				if firstNonApprove == nil {
515					firstNonApprove = res
516				}
517			}
518		}
519		if firstNonApprove != nil {
520			return firstNonApprove, nil
521		}
522		return firstApprove, nil
523	}
524	
525	func reviewResultHasACEvidence(res *ReviewResult) bool {
526		if res == nil || res.Verdict != VerdictApprove {
527			return false
528		}
529		if len(res.PerAC) == 0 {
530			return false
531		}
532		for _, ac := range res.PerAC {
533			if strings.TrimSpace(ac.Evidence) == "" {
534				return false
535			}
536		}
537		return true
538	}
539	
540	// applyReviewTriageDecision runs the triage policy after a BLOCK or
541	// REQUEST_CHANGES review verdict has been recorded for `beadID`. It reads
542	// prior review events to compute the BLOCK count, biases the result toward
543	// re-attempt when the latest BLOCK was paired with a degraded reviewer
544	// (kind:review-pairing-degraded), then applies the chosen action by recording
545	// triage-decision evidence and, for operator-required, parking with legacy
546	// cleanup.
547	//
548	// Errors from the underlying store are best-effort: callers continue
549	// regardless so a triage-side failure cannot strand a bead in an
550	// inconsistent review state. This callout previously lived as a method on
551	// ExecuteBeadWorker; the C3
552	// extraction (ddx-a921ff01) moves it inside the post-merge-review pipeline
553	// driven by RunPostMergeReview / try.Attempt.
554	func applyReviewTriageDecision(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, currentPowerClass string) error {
555		events, err := store.Events(beadID)
556		if err != nil {
557			return err
558		}
559	
560		var blockTimestamps []time.Time
561		var pairingDegraded []time.Time
562		for _, ev := range events {
563			switch {
564			case ev.Kind == "review" && strings.TrimSpace(ev.Summary) == "BLOCK":
565				blockTimestamps = append(blockTimestamps, ev.CreatedAt)
566			case ev.Kind == ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind:
567				pairingDegraded = append(pairingDegraded, ev.CreatedAt)
568			}
569		}
570	
571		// History excludes the current attempt. The just-appended BLOCK (if any)
572		// is the last entry in blockTimestamps and must not be counted as prior
573		// history per triage.Decide()'s contract.
574		historyCount := len(blockTimestamps)
575		if historyCount > 0 {
576			historyCount--
577		}
578		history := make([]triage.FailureMode, 0, historyCount)
579		for i := 0; i < historyCount; i++ {
580			history = append(history, triage.FailureModeReviewBlock)
581		}
582	
583		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
584		action := policy.Decide(beadID, triage.FailureModeReviewBlock, history)
585	
586		pairedDegraded := latestBlockPairedDegraded(blockTimestamps, pairingDegraded)
587		if pairedDegraded && action == triage.ActionEscalatePower {
588			action = triage.ActionReAttemptWithContext
589		}
590	
591		return applyTriageAction(store, beadID, actor, now, action, currentPowerClass, pairedDegraded)
592	}
593	
594	// latestBlockPairedDegraded reports whether the most recent BLOCK event was
595	// paired with a review-pairing-degraded event from the same attempt window
596	// (between the previous BLOCK and this one).
597	func latestBlockPairedDegraded(blocks, pairing []time.Time) bool {
598		if len(blocks) == 0 || len(pairing) == 0 {
599			return false
600		}
601		latest := blocks[len(blocks)-1]
602		var prev time.Time
603		if len(blocks) >= 2 {
604			prev = blocks[len(blocks)-2]
605		}
606		for _, t := range pairing {
607			if t.After(prev) && !t.After(latest) {
608				return true
609			}
610		}
611		return false
612	}
613	
614	// applyTriageAction performs the side effects for a chosen Action. Review-block
615	// escalation is attempt-scoped evidence only, recorded on the triage-decision
616	// event body. Operator-required outcomes may park the bead to proposed while
617	// clearing stale legacy retry-hint metadata, and every path records a
618	// triage-decision event.
619	func applyTriageAction(store ExecuteBeadLoopStore, beadID, actor string, now time.Time, action triage.Action, currentPowerClass string, pairedDegraded bool) error {
620		body := map[string]any{
621			"action": string(action),
622			"mode":   string(triage.FailureModeReviewBlock),
623		}
624		if pairedDegraded {
625			body["pairing_degraded"] = true
626		}
627	
628		switch action {
629		case triage.ActionEscalatePower:
630			nextPowerClass := nextEscalatedPowerClass(currentPowerClass)
631			body["next_power_class"] = string(nextPowerClass)
632		case triage.ActionOperatorRequired:
633			if err := store.ParkToProposed(beadID, bead.ParkPostReviewMalfunction, func(b *bead.Bead) {
634				if b.Extra == nil {
635					b.Extra = make(map[string]any)
636				}
637				b.Labels = removeBeadLabels(b.Labels, TriageNeedsHumanLabel, bead.LabelNeedsHuman, bead.LabelNeedsInvestigation)
638				clearReviewTriageClaimMetadata(b)
639				bead.SetNeedsHumanMeta(b, bead.NeedsHumanMeta{
640					Reason:          "review BLOCK triage reached operator-required rung",
641					Since:           now.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
642					Source:          "ddx work",
643					SuggestedAction: "review the blocked attempt and accept, split, block, or cancel",
644					Summary:         "review BLOCK triage requires operator decision",
645				})
646			}); err != nil {
647				return err
648			}
649		}
650	
651		bodyJSON, _ := json.Marshal(body)
652		return store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
653			Kind:      triageDecisionEventKind,
654			Summary:   string(triage.FailureModeReviewBlock) + ": " + string(action),
655			Body:      string(bodyJSON),
656			Actor:     actor,
657			Source:    "ddx work",
658			CreatedAt: now,
659		})
660	}
661	
662	func clearReviewTriageClaimMetadata(b *bead.Bead) {
663		b.Owner = ""
664		if b.Extra == nil {
665			return
666		}
667		for _, key := range bead.ClaimMetadataExtraKeys {
668			delete(b.Extra, key)
669		}
670		delete(b.Extra, bead.ClaimHeartbeatExtraKey)
671	}
672	
673	// nextEscalatedPowerClass returns the next powerClass above `current`. An unrecognised or
674	// empty current powerClass defaults to standard; smart is its own ceiling.
675	func nextEscalatedPowerClass(current string) escalation.PowerClass {
676		switch escalation.PowerClass(current) {
677		case escalation.PowerCheap:
678			return escalation.PowerStandard
679		case escalation.PowerStandard:
680			return escalation.PowerSmart
681		case escalation.PowerSmart:
682			return escalation.PowerSmart
683		default:
684			return escalation.PowerStandard
685		}
686	}

1	// Package attemptmetrics implements the cold-powerClass metrics layer: one row per
2	// bead-attempt written to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl. Rows are appended with
3	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer access is safe. The schema_version field
4	// allows future migrations without losing historical rows.
5	package attemptmetrics
6	
7	import (
8		"bufio"
9		"encoding/json"
10		"os"
11		"path/filepath"
12		"time"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
16	)
17	
18	const SchemaVersion = 1
19	
20	// AttemptRow is one row in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
21	type AttemptRow struct {
22		SchemaVersion int    `json:"schema_version"`
23		AttemptID     string `json:"attempt_id"`
24		BeadID        string `json:"bead_id"`
25		SessionID     string `json:"session_id,omitempty"`
26		TSStart       string `json:"ts_start,omitempty"` // RFC3339
27		TSEnd         string `json:"ts_end,omitempty"`   // RFC3339
28	
29		Model    string `json:"model,omitempty"`
30		Harness  string `json:"harness,omitempty"`
31		Profile  string `json:"profile,omitempty"`
32		Provider string `json:"provider,omitempty"`
33	
34		// PromptTemplateID and PromptTemplateHash identify the prompt variant used.
35		PromptTemplateID   string `json:"prompt_template_id,omitempty"`
36		PromptTemplateHash string `json:"prompt_template_hash,omitempty"` // hex sha256 of rendered prompt
37	
38		SpecID string `json:"spec_id,omitempty"`
39	
40		Outcome  string  `json:"outcome"`
41		ExitCode int     `json:"exit_code"`
42		CostUSD  float64 `json:"cost_usd,omitempty"`
43	
44		DurationMS   int `json:"duration_ms"`
45		InputTokens  int `json:"input_tokens,omitempty"`
46		OutputTokens int `json:"output_tokens,omitempty"`
47		TotalTokens  int `json:"total_tokens,omitempty"`
48	
49		ReviewVerdict                string         `json:"review_verdict,omitempty"`
50		ReviewFindingCountBySeverity map[string]int `json:"review_finding_count_by_severity,omitempty"`
51	
52		// ReviewerModel is the resolved model used for the reviewer dispatch.
53		ReviewerModel string `json:"reviewer_model,omitempty"`
54		// ReviewerACMismatchCount is the number of ACs where the reviewer's grade
55		// disagreed with the ac-check.json mechanical result.
56		ReviewerACMismatchCount int `json:"reviewer_acmismatch_count,omitempty"`
57		// ReviewerOverrideReasons lists each AC where reviewer and ac-check diverged.
58		ReviewerOverrideReasons []string `json:"reviewer_override_reasons,omitempty"`
59		// ReviewerAccuracySignal records the eventual truth after the reviewer's
60		// verdict: confirmed (operator agreed), override (operator contradicted),
61		// contested, or unknown (not yet determined).
62		ReviewerAccuracySignal string `json:"reviewer_accuracy_signal,omitempty"`
63	
64		LadderMinPowerInitial int `json:"ladder_min_power_initial,omitempty"`
65		LadderMinPowerFinal   int `json:"ladder_min_power_final,omitempty"`
66		LadderStepsTaken      int `json:"ladder_steps_taken,omitempty"`
67	
68		// ReplayOf, when non-empty, identifies the original attempt_id this row
69		// was produced from. Set by `ddx bead replay` so the analyze CLI can
70		// group replays with their source attempt.
71		ReplayOf string `json:"replay_of,omitempty"`
72	}
73	
74	// BeadAttemptEvents is the raw input for BackfillFromEvents: a bead ID, its
75	// optional spec-id, and its full event stream.
76	type BeadAttemptEvents struct {
77		BeadID string
78		SpecID string // from bead Extra["spec-id"], optional
79		Events []bead.BeadEvent
80	}
81	
82	// costEventBody matches the JSON shape written by appendBeadCostEvidence.
83	type costEventBody struct {
84		AttemptID    string  `json:"attempt_id"`
85		Harness      string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
86		Provider     string  `json:"provider,omitempty"`
87		Model        string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
88		InputTokens  int     `json:"input_tokens"`
89		OutputTokens int     `json:"output_tokens"`
90		TotalTokens  int     `json:"total_tokens"`
91		CostUSD      float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
92		DurationMS   int     `json:"duration_ms"`
93		ExitCode     int     `json:"exit_code"`
94	}
95	
96	// AttemptsPath returns the absolute path to metrics/attempts.jsonl under the
97	// project's DDx state root.
98	func AttemptsPath(projectRoot string) string {
99		return ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, "metrics", "attempts.jsonl")
100	}
101	
102	// AppendRow appends row to .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl under projectRoot using
103	// O_APPEND so concurrent single-writer appends are safe. Creates parent
104	// directories if needed. Best-effort callers may discard the returned error.
105	func AppendRow(projectRoot string, row AttemptRow) error {
106		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
107		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
108			return err
109		}
110		f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o644)
111		if err != nil {
112			return err
113		}
114		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
115		data, err := json.Marshal(row)
116		if err != nil {
117			return err
118		}
119		data = append(data, '\n')
120		_, err = f.Write(data)
121		return err
122	}
123	
124	// LoadAttemptIDs reads .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl and returns the set of
125	// attempt_ids already present. Used to deduplicate BackfillFromEvents runs.
126	// Returns an empty map when the file does not exist.
127	func LoadAttemptIDs(projectRoot string) (map[string]struct{}, error) {
128		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
129		f, err := os.Open(path)
130		if err != nil {
131			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
132				return map[string]struct{}{}, nil
133			}
134			return nil, err
135		}
136		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
137	
138		seen := map[string]struct{}{}
139		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
140		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 1024*1024), 1024*1024) // 1 MiB lines max
141		for sc.Scan() {
142			var row struct {
143				AttemptID string `json:"attempt_id"`
144			}
145			if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &row); err != nil || row.AttemptID == "" {
146				continue
147			}
148			seen[row.AttemptID] = struct{}{}
149		}
150		return seen, sc.Err()
151	}
152	
153	// LoadRows reads all rows from .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl. Malformed lines
154	// are skipped. Returns an empty slice when the file does not exist.
155	func LoadRows(projectRoot string) ([]AttemptRow, error) {
156		path := AttemptsPath(projectRoot)
157		f, err := os.Open(path)
158		if err != nil {
159			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
160				return []AttemptRow{}, nil
161			}
162			return nil, err
163		}
164		defer f.Close() //nolint:errcheck
165	
166		var rows []AttemptRow
167		sc := bufio.NewScanner(f)
168		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 1024*1024), 1024*1024)
169		for sc.Scan() {
170			var row AttemptRow
171			if err := json.Unmarshal(sc.Bytes(), &row); err != nil {
172				continue
173			}
174			rows = append(rows, row)
175		}
176		return rows, sc.Err()
177	}
178	
179	// BackfillFromEvents processes beads' event streams, appending one AttemptRow
180	// per attempt found (identified by kind:cost events). Deduplicates by
181	// attempt_id: rows already in attempts.jsonl are skipped. Returns the count of
182	// new rows added.
183	//
184	// The matching algorithm: for each kind:cost event, scan forward to find the
185	// next kind:execute-bead event (which carries the finalization outcome). This
186	// pair gives the attempt's cost/token/timing data and its loop-level outcome.
187	func BackfillFromEvents(projectRoot string, beads []BeadAttemptEvents) (int, error) {
188		seen, err := LoadAttemptIDs(projectRoot)
189		if err != nil {
190			return 0, err
191		}
192	
193		var added int
194		for _, b := range beads {
195			rows := extractRowsFromEvents(b)
196			for _, row := range rows {
197				if _, exists := seen[row.AttemptID]; exists {
198					continue
199				}
200				if err := AppendRow(projectRoot, row); err != nil {
201					return added, err
202				}
203				seen[row.AttemptID] = struct{}{}
204				added++
205			}
206		}
207		return added, nil
208	}
209	
210	// extractRowsFromEvents derives one AttemptRow per kind:cost event found in
211	// b.Events. Each cost event is paired with the immediately following
212	// kind:execute-bead event to obtain the outcome.
213	func extractRowsFromEvents(b BeadAttemptEvents) []AttemptRow {
214		var rows []AttemptRow
215		for i, ev := range b.Events {
216			if ev.Kind != "cost" {
217				continue
218			}
219			var cost costEventBody
220			if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &cost); err != nil || cost.AttemptID == "" {
221				continue
222			}
223	
224			// Scan forward for the next execute-bead event to get outcome.
225			outcome := ""
226			for _, next := range b.Events[i+1:] {
227				if next.Kind == "execute-bead" {
228					outcome = next.Summary
229					break
230				}
231			}
232	
233			tsEnd := ""
234			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
235				tsEnd = ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
236			}
237			// Approximate ts_start from ts_end and duration.
238			tsStart := ""
239			if !ev.CreatedAt.IsZero() && cost.DurationMS > 0 {
240				start := ev.CreatedAt.UTC().Add(-time.Duration(cost.DurationMS) * time.Millisecond)
241				tsStart = start.Format(time.RFC3339)
242			}
243	
244			total := cost.TotalTokens
245			if total == 0 {
246				total = cost.InputTokens + cost.OutputTokens
247			}
248	
249			row := AttemptRow{
250				SchemaVersion: SchemaVersion,
251				AttemptID:     cost.AttemptID,
252				BeadID:        b.BeadID,
253				SpecID:        b.SpecID,
254				TSStart:       tsStart,
255				TSEnd:         tsEnd,
256				Model:         cost.Model,
257				Harness:       cost.Harness,
258				Provider:      cost.Provider,
259				Outcome:       outcome,
260				ExitCode:      cost.ExitCode,
261				CostUSD:       cost.CostUSD,
262				DurationMS:    cost.DurationMS,
263				InputTokens:   cost.InputTokens,
264				OutputTokens:  cost.OutputTokens,
265				TotalTokens:   total,
266			}
267			rows = append(rows, row)
268		}
269		return rows
270	}
271	
272	// Rfc3339 formats a time.Time as RFC3339 UTC; returns "" for the zero value.
273	func Rfc3339(t time.Time) string {
274		if t.IsZero() {
275			return ""
276		}
277		return t.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
278	}

7	// MarkResultExecutionError preserves a populated worker result as a structured
8	// attempt failure. Callers use this when ExecuteBeadWithConfig returned both a
9	// result and an error; the presence of the result means DDx has enough
10	// execution context to report the attempt instead of aborting the process.
11	func MarkResultExecutionError(res *ExecuteBeadResult, err error) {
12		if res == nil || err == nil {
13			return
14		}
15		if res.Outcome == "" {
16			res.Outcome = ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed
17		}
18		if res.Reason == "" {
19			res.Reason = err.Error()
20		}
21		if res.Error == "" {
22			res.Error = err.Error()
23		}
24		if res.Status == "" || res.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges {
25			res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus("error", res.ExitCode, res.Reason)
26		}
27		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
28		res.FailureMode = ClassifyFailureMode(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Error)
29		if res.FailureMode == "" {
30			res.FailureMode = FailureModeUnknown
31		}

34	// MarkResultLandError records a coordinator/landing failure on a completed
35	// worker result. When possible it first pins ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations
36	// so the worker's commit remains reachable even though the target branch did
37	// not advance cleanly.
38	func MarkResultLandError(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult, err error) {
39		if res == nil || err == nil {
40			return
41		}
42		reason := "land coordination failed: " + err.Error()

44			preserveRef := landIterationRef(res.BeadID, res.AttemptID, res.BaseRev)
45			if upErr := (RealLandingGitOps{}).UpdateRefTo(projectRoot, preserveRef, res.ResultRev, ""); upErr == nil {

48				reason = fmt.Sprintf("%s; preserving result failed: %v", reason, upErr)
49			}

51		res.Outcome = "error"
52		res.Reason = reason

54		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, res.Reason)

   - This ensures the terminal verdict is guaranteed to survive in the report for all verdict paths

2. **Created `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review_test.go`**:
   - Added 4 comprehensive regression tests covering:
     - APPROVE verdict path: verifies report contains verdict, event is recorded, metrics round-trip
     - REQUEST_CHANGES verdict path: verifies report contains verdict and event is recorded
     - All verdict paths preserve and return report with ReviewVerdict set
